Investor Pitch — July 2026

FAIRWAY
GOLF CLUB

The First AI-Powered Golf Improvement Club

Northern Virginia  |  fairwaygolfclub.co

The Vision

Every visit makes you
a better golfer.

Not another dark simulator rental. A premium, AI-powered golf improvement club where technology remembers every shot, coaches you between visits, and builds a community of golfers who actually get better.

Let’s walk through a visit…

Step 1: Arrival

You see it from the parking lot.

Gold lettering on historic red brick. Warm light through glass. A couple walks in with clubs. This isn’t a warehouse — it’s a place you want to be.

Northern Virginia

Step 2: The Entrance

First impression: this is different.

Black-framed glass doors. The wordmark in gold above. Through the glass you see walnut, warm pendants, and a reception desk. You know instantly — this isn’t the dark simulator spot you tried last year.

Step 3: Check-In

Tap in. The system knows you’re here.

iPad kiosk. Big screen: “Welcome back, Russ — Bay 2 is ready.” No waiting, no paperwork. Golfer360 loaded. AI coach has today’s plan queued.

Salesforce Experience Cloud + Agentforce

Step 4: Your AI Coach

“Hey Russ — here’s what we’re working on today.”

Bay monitor greets you: “Driver accuracy up 12% last time. Today: 7-iron — you’re 8 yards short of target.” Every session starts where the last one left off.

Agentforce + Data Cloud

Step 5: The Lounge

Drop your bag. Grab a beer. Settle in.

Chesterfield leather. View through glass into the bays. Scorecards from last night’s league. You’re early — and that’s the point. People come early. People stay late.

Step 6: The Bar

Craft beer from next door. Literally.

Bunny Man Brewing is 50 feet away. Their beer on our taps. Beer flights. Leaderboard on the TV. Where golfers become regulars and regulars become members.

Step 7: Your Bay

Every shot captured. Every session smarter.

Launch monitor tracks angle, speed, spin, carry. Feeds Golfer360 automatically. Amber lighting. Quality turf. AI coach adjusts your plan in real time.

Step 8: The Scene

Two bays. Two groups. One energy.

A woman on her driver in Bay 1. Three friends with beers in Bay 2. The “Now Playing” board shows scores. Competition. Community. Why this works.

Step 9: The Lesson

Human expertise meets AI analysis.

Instructor has your full history. Swing replay with overlays. Launch data beside it. This lesson builds on every session you’ve ever had here.

The Details

Automatic ball return. Zero interruptions.

Illuminated tee feeds balls automatically. No bending, no rhythm break. The feature every competitor visit taught us matters most.

The Details

Brick. Leather. Walnut. Warm light.

Golf books on shelves. St. Andrews on the wall. Every detail says: you belong here. This is your club. Not a rental. A place.

The Details

Golf-themed taps. Branded everything.

Custom tap handles. Chalkboard menu. Fairway coaster on live-edge bar. Every touchpoint reinforces the brand. This is how you build something people wear on a hat.

Step 10: After Your Session

You came to hit balls. You stayed for the experience.

Beer flight. Scorecard. Pendant lights. Your app: “7-iron carry improved 4 yards this month.” You’ll be back Thursday.

Step 11: On Your Way Out

You grab a headcover. Because you’re a member now.

Branded headcovers, hats, gloves on walnut display wall. Members get 15%. Every item that leaves is a billboard.

Why It Works: The Flywheel
The Technology Moat

Play. Capture. Analyze. Coach. Improve. Return.

Every visit feeds the AI. The AI makes the next visit better. Better visits mean higher retention. Higher retention means more data. Competitors can’t catch up — we have the compounding data advantage.

Chapter

FOR INVESTORS

Market opportunity, financials, and the ask.

Market: Zero Competition Zone
Competitive Positioning
Technology Platform

Built on Salesforce by a Salesforce professional.

  • Salesforce CRM — Member management, booking, communications
  • Data Cloud — Unified golfer profiles from every session
  • Agentforce AI — Personalized coaching and recommendations
  • Tableau — Performance dashboards for members
  • Experience Cloud — Member portal and self-service booking
  • Automation — Lights, HVAC, booking — minimal staff needed
Revenue Model

Seven streams from day one.

Bay Rentals$55-70/hrWalk-in and online booking
Memberships$99-299/moBronze/Silver/Gold tiers — recurring
AI Coaching$49/moAgentforce-powered improvement plans
Corporate$500-2KTeam building, client entertainment
Leagues$200-500Per season — community, retention
Craft Beer$6-9/pourBunny Man Brewing partnership
Mobile Events$500-1.5KFairway Mobile unit — events + lead gen
Year 1 Revenue Build-Up — Club + Mobile
Fairway Mobile

A portable golf club. A marketing machine.

One SUV. One person. Sets up in 20 minutes. Gets PAID to collect leads. Every event fills the pipeline back at the club.

Branded SUV — everything fits in the back

Fairway Mobile: The Event

Corporate parties. Fundraisers. Weddings. Community days.

Guests swing, get a Golfer360 profile on the spot, and leave with a reason to visit the club. 50–100 contacts per event. $500–$1,500 per booking.

Fairway Mobile: The Rig

Under $5K. Pays for itself in 5 events.

Portable simulator (~$2K) + inflatable screen + pump (~$2K) + projector + iPad + hitting mat. Fits in one vehicle. One person operates it.

Salesforce captures every contact → automated nurture → club membership

Fairway Mobile: The Numbers

Revenue stream + marketing channel in one.

Phased Growth

Start lean. Prove demand. Expand from profits.

Phase 1 — Validate (Year 1)

  • 2 premium simulator bays
  • $150K total investment
  • $55/hr, 12 hrs/day
  • 2 part-time staff
  • Mobile unit active
  • Founder maintains full-time career

Phase 2 — Scale (Year 3)

  • Expand to 4 bays
  • $100K additional investment
  • 65%+ utilization proven
  • Full-time staff model
  • Founder transitions to CEO
  • Franchise model exploration

Founding Investor Perks

Your name goes on the wall. Literally.

  • 8% Annual Return — Paid monthly into your account. Beats the market without the volatility.
  • Lifetime Founding Membership — Unlimited bay time during off-peak hours. Bring a guest anytime.
  • Founders Wall — Your name in the club. Permanent. You were here first.
  • Priority Booking — Reserve peak hours 48 hours before the public.
  • 20% Off Everything — Events, lessons, merchandise, beer. Always.
  • First Right to Phase 2 — When we expand to 4 bays, you get the call before anyone else.
Why This Founder

Unfair advantages that compound.

  • Salesforce Expert — The technology platform IS my day job. No learning curve.
  • Disciplined Operator — Conservative, phased approach. No desperation, only discipline.
  • Local Ownership — Lives minutes from the location. No absentee operator risk.
  • Golfer + Customer — Built what I want to use. I understand the frustration firsthand.
  • Automation Mindset — 2 part-time staff, not 10. Technology replaces headcount.
Brand: Ready to Launch

THE PLAN

How this becomes YOUR exit strategy.

Personal
The Big Picture

This isn’t me chasing a dream.
This is us building your freedom.

The goal isn’t for me to quit my job. The goal is to build a business that throws off enough cash that you can quit yours first — on your terms, on your timeline, with zero financial stress.

I keep grinding. The business grows. You get your life back.

Personal
Why This Is Safe

Six layers of protection.

  • Both jobs stay — nobody quits anything in Phase 1. Purely additive income.
  • Only $75K equity at risk — loan secured by equipment, not our home.
  • Cash-flow positive in 3 months — business feeds itself immediately.
  • 1 mile from home — manage evenings/weekends, zero commute.
  • Automated operation — 2 part-time staff. Not demanding full attention.
  • Worst case — sell sims (~$40K), exit lease, net loss ~$35K. Painful, not life-altering.

Personal
The Decision

What I’m asking for.

The Cost (Short Term)

  • $75K equity + $75K loan (+$5K mobile)
  • Some evenings/weekends at the club
  • Loan payment (~$1,520/mo) covered by revenue

The Return (Long Term)

  • Year 2: investment recovered
  • Year 4: I quit — it’s a raise
  • Year 5: YOU quit — business replaces your salary
  • Year 6+: $2.5M–$5M asset we own

If it works: you never work for someone else again. If not: we lose ~$35K, both still have careers, and we tried.
